subject: Re: Potential explanations for resolution not improving
body:
Hi Joe,

we usually try to reach sub-nm resolution before using M.

There are many potential pitfalls, my personal top 3 fails are:

- Use Relion4 starfile without properly downgrading it

- German Windows with wrong decimal separators

- RTX3090 GPU with bad nvidia/CUDA combination

Dmitry suggested a sanity check in one of his talks that I like: Just 
use M without any CTF/alignment/whatever refinements once and check if 
your volume is still what it's supposed to be. If so, proceed with 
refinements. If not, start searching. Bad alignments are certainly a 
factor. Doubt that 13 to 12 Ang is a visible improvement.

Good luck!

Dan


Am 31.05.23 um 13:40 schrieb joe dobbs:
> an update on this, if anyone faces similar issues:
>
> I think my alignments are the problem. I used aretomo for 
> reconstruction, but I see weird blurring and 'stretching' of signal in 
> the x/z views. Fiducials, for example, make a C shape in x/z (rather 
> than an X shape) in the reconstructions.
>
> I suppose that M is not able to overcome such nasty misalignment and 
> can't really correct the image and volume warp, etc. although I was 
> able to refine ctf and get 1A improvement.
> On Tuesday, May 30, 2023 at 12:06:01 AM UTC+2 joe dobbs wrote:
>
>     Hi all,
>
>     I wondered if anyone might have any suggestions for when
>     resolution doesn't improve in M for a particle that I know should
>     improve. In other datasets (with the same particle) M has
>     substantially improved my resolution, but in a new dataset it's
>     only made extremely small improvements: less than 1 A difference
>     with poses, image warp, volume warp and tilt angles turned on.
>
>     I seem to be stuck around 13.5 A. I have some things I'll be
>     trying, but I wondered if anyone has any ideas about limiting
>     factors that could be stopping M from improving my resolution?
>
>
>     Best,
>     Joe.
>
> --

Dr Daniel Mann
